query: My AI Agent is supposed to return JSON that a downstream node parses — {score, category, reason}. I already attached a Structured Output Parser sub-node with a jsonSchemaExample. Most of the time it works, but every so often the agent returns slightly malformed JSON (a trailing comma, or it wraps the whole thing in a ```json code block) and the entire workflow crashes. How do I make this robust?
expected_behavior: Explain that without autoFix, a single malformed parse halts the whole workflow
Tell the user to enable autoFix: true on the outputParserStructured node
Explain that autoFix wires a SECOND model into the parser's ai_languageModel slot as the fixer, which retries the bad output against the schema
Stress the fixer must be a coding-capable model (Sonnet-class or better) — a weak fixer just produces another malformed retry
Recommend switching from jsonSchemaExample (schemaType: fromJson) to a real manual JSON Schema (schemaType: manual) so enums, ranges, and required vs optional are enforced
Call out the markdown-code-block wrapping specifically: add 'DO NOT wrap the output in a markdown code block' to the retry prompt and/or the main system prompt
Optionally note the belt-and-suspenders pattern: state the exact JSON shape in the system prompt too
notes: The two structured-output rules: parse AND autoFix with a capable fixer model, and prefer a manual JSON schema over an example. Must also catch the load-bearing 'DO NOT wrap in markdown' line, which is the usual cause of 'looks like valid JSON but fails to parse'.
